questionText: A drawing application must render the same shapes on multiple platforms (OpenGL, DirectX, SVG), and you want the shape abstraction and the rendering implementation to evolve independently without a class explosion. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Decoupling an abstraction (shape) from its implementation (renderer) so both vary independently is the Bridge pattern.

questionText: Complete the Factory Method: ForestLevel must override the creator of LevelFactory.
explanation: The creator declares an abstract factory method; the concrete creator extends it and returns the concrete product.

questionText: Match each design pattern to the intent that best describes it.
matchingOptions: Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
Attaches additional responsibilities to an object dynamically by wrapping it
Provides a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it
Creates new objects by cloning an existing instance instead of instantiating from scratch

correctAnswers.Decorator: Attaches additional responsibilities to an object dynamically by wrapping it
correctAnswers.Facade: Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
correctAnswers.Prototype: Creates new objects by cloning an existing instance instead of instantiating from scratch
correctAnswers.Proxy: Provides a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: An air-traffic control simulation has many aircraft that must coordinate, but you want to avoid every aircraft holding direct references to every other; instead they communicate through a central control tower. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Centralising many-to-many communication through one coordinator object to reduce coupling is the Mediator pattern.

questionText: Complete the Factory Method: PdfApp must override the creator of DocumentApp.
explanation: The creator declares an abstract factory method; the concrete creator extends it and returns the concrete product.

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their defining intent.
matchingOptions: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
Shares common intrinsic state between many fine-grained objects to save memory
Defines the skeleton of an algorithm in a base method, deferring some steps to subclasses
Centralizes communication between a set of objects so they no longer refer to each other directly

correctAnswers.Flyweight: Shares common intrinsic state between many fine-grained objects to save memory
correctAnswers.Mediator: Centralizes communication between a set of objects so they no longer refer to each other directly
correctAnswers.Simple Factory: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
correctAnswers.Template Method: Defines the skeleton of an algorithm in a base method, deferring some steps to subclasses

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: A music player must let users iterate through the current playlist (next/previous) without exposing how the tracks are stored internally, and must let users switch the play order algorithm (sequential, shuffle, repeat) at runtime. Which design patterns best fit? Select 1 or more answers
explanation: Sequential access without exposing internal storage is Iterator; runtime-swappable ordering algorithms are Strategy.

questionText: Complete the Factory Method: SmsCreator must override the creator of NotificationCreator.
explanation: The creator declares an abstract factory method; the concrete creator extends it and returns the concrete product.

questionText: For each design pattern below, select the statement that captures its purpose.
matchingOptions: Defines an interface for creating an object but lets subclasses decide which class to instantiate
Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime
Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem

correctAnswers.Adapter: Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
correctAnswers.Composite: Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
correctAnswers.Facade: Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
correctAnswers.Factory Method: Defines an interface for creating an object but lets subclasses decide which class to instantiate
correctAnswers.Simple Factory: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
correctAnswers.Strategy: Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: A reporting tool must add a new export operation (to XML, then later to LaTeX) across an existing hierarchy of document-element classes (paragraph, table, image) WITHOUT modifying those element classes each time a new export format is added. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Adding new operations over a fixed set of element classes without changing them is the Visitor pattern (double dispatch).

questionText: Complete the Observer pattern where Stock notifies its Investor objects.
explanation: The subject keeps a list of observers and calls update() on each one when it changes.

questionText: Associate every design pattern with the goal it is meant to achieve.
matchingOptions: Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
Encapsulates a request as an object, allowing parameterization, queuing, and undo
Passes a request along a chain of handlers until one of them handles it
Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
Provides an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes

correctAnswers.Abstract Factory: Provides an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes
correctAnswers.Adapter: Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
correctAnswers.Chain of Responsibility: Passes a request along a chain of handlers until one of them handles it
correctAnswers.Command: Encapsulates a request as an object, allowing parameterization, queuing, and undo
correctAnswers.Composite: Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: A simple calculator app must parse and evaluate arithmetic expressions written in a small custom language such as '3 + 4 * 2'. Which single design pattern is most associated with defining and evaluating such a grammar?
explanation: Defining a grammar and an evaluator for sentences in a small language is the Interpreter pattern.

questionText: Complete the Observer pattern where NewsAgency notifies its Subscriber objects.
explanation: The subject keeps a list of observers and calls update() on each one when it changes.

questionText: Match each design pattern to the intent that best describes it.
matchingOptions: Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
Decouples an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently
Provides an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes
Separates the construction of a complex object from its representation using a step-by-step process
Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class

correctAnswers.Abstract Factory: Provides an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes
correctAnswers.Bridge: Decouples an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently
correctAnswers.Builder: Separates the construction of a complex object from its representation using a step-by-step process
correctAnswers.Composite: Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
correctAnswers.State: Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: An expense-approval workflow escalates a request up a chain: a team lead can approve up to $1,000, a manager up to $10,000, and a director above that; the first level with sufficient authority handles it. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Passing a request along a chain until a handler with enough authority processes it is Chain of Responsibility.

questionText: Complete the Observer pattern where WeatherStation notifies its Display objects.
explanation: The subject keeps a list of observers and calls update() on each one when it changes.

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their defining intent.
matchingOptions: Ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global access point to it
Defines a one-to-many dependency so that when one object changes state all its dependents are notified automatically
Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class
Provides a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it

correctAnswers.Observer: Defines a one-to-many dependency so that when one object changes state all its dependents are notified automatically
correctAnswers.Proxy: Provides a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it
correctAnswers.Singleton: Ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global access point to it
correctAnswers.State: Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: A travel-booking site must offer one simple bookTrip() call that internally coordinates separate flight, hotel and car-rental subsystems, hiding their complexity from the client. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: A single unified interface that hides a complex subsystem is the Facade pattern.

questionText: Complete the Observer pattern where Button notifies its Listener objects.
explanation: The subject keeps a list of observers and calls update() on each one when it changes.

questionText: For each design pattern below, select the statement that captures its purpose.
matchingOptions: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
Captures and externalizes an object's internal state so it can be restored later without violating encapsulation
Decouples an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently
Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class

correctAnswers.Bridge: Decouples an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently
correctAnswers.Composite: Composes objects into tree structures and lets clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly
correctAnswers.Facade: Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
correctAnswers.Memento: Captures and externalizes an object's internal state so it can be restored later without violating encapsulation
correctAnswers.Simple Factory: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
correctAnswers.State: Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: A coffee-shop ordering system starts from a base beverage and lets customers stack any number of optional add-ons (milk, syrup, whipped cream), each adding to the price and description, in any combination. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Dynamically stacking optional responsibilities onto a base object by wrapping is the Decorator pattern.

questionText: Complete the Observer pattern where GameServer notifies its Player objects.
explanation: The subject keeps a list of observers and calls update() on each one when it changes.

questionText: Associate every design pattern with the goal it is meant to achieve.
matchingOptions: Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
Captures and externalizes an object's internal state so it can be restored later without violating encapsulation
Lets you define a new operation on a set of objects without changing the classes of those objects
Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime
Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
Centralizes communication between a set of objects so they no longer refer to each other directly

correctAnswers.Adapter: Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
correctAnswers.Facade: Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
correctAnswers.Mediator: Centralizes communication between a set of objects so they no longer refer to each other directly
correctAnswers.Memento: Captures and externalizes an object's internal state so it can be restored later without violating encapsulation
correctAnswers.Strategy: Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime
correctAnswers.Visitor: Lets you define a new operation on a set of objects without changing the classes of those objects

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: A database access library must ensure that only one connection pool object exists across the entire application, lazily created the first time it is requested. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Guaranteeing a single, globally accessible, lazily created instance is the Singleton pattern.

questionText: Complete the Strategy pattern: Checkout delegates to a swappable PaymentStrategy.
explanation: The context holds a strategy reference, lets it be replaced, and delegates the algorithm to it.

questionText: Match each design pattern to the intent that best describes it.
matchingOptions: Attaches additional responsibilities to an object dynamically by wrapping it
Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime
Shares common intrinsic state between many fine-grained objects to save memory
Creates new objects by cloning an existing instance instead of instantiating from scratch
Ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global access point to it

correctAnswers.Decorator: Attaches additional responsibilities to an object dynamically by wrapping it
correctAnswers.Flyweight: Shares common intrinsic state between many fine-grained objects to save memory
correctAnswers.Prototype: Creates new objects by cloning an existing instance instead of instantiating from scratch
correctAnswers.Singleton: Ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global access point to it
correctAnswers.Strategy: Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: An IoT platform receives raw readings from many sensor vendors, each with an incompatible SDK, but the analytics engine expects one common SensorReading interface. The platform must also keep exactly one MQTT broker client. Which design patterns best fit? Select 1 or more answers
explanation: Wrapping each vendor SDK to the common interface is Adapter; the single shared broker client is Singleton.

questionText: Complete the Strategy pattern: Sorter delegates to a swappable SortStrategy.
explanation: The context holds a strategy reference, lets it be replaced, and delegates the algorithm to it.

questionText: Group the following design patterns with their defining intent.
matchingOptions: Decouples an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently
Provides a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it
Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class
Lets you define a new operation on a set of objects without changing the classes of those objects

correctAnswers.Bridge: Decouples an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently
correctAnswers.Proxy: Provides a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it
correctAnswers.Simple Factory: Encapsulates object creation in one method or class that returns instances based on a parameter
correctAnswers.State: Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class
correctAnswers.Visitor: Lets you define a new operation on a set of objects without changing the classes of those objects

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: A UI theming engine must create coordinated sets of components for several themes (Material, Cupertino, Fluent), and the whole app must obtain its theme objects from a single theme manager that exists only once. Which design patterns best fit? Select 1 or more answers
explanation: Coordinated families of theme components are Abstract Factory; one theme manager instance is Singleton.

questionText: Complete the Strategy pattern: Compressor delegates to a swappable CompressionStrategy.
explanation: The context holds a strategy reference, lets it be replaced, and delegates the algorithm to it.

questionText: For each design pattern below, select the statement that captures its purpose.
matchingOptions: Provides an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes
Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime
Creates new objects by cloning an existing instance instead of instantiating from scratch
Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect

correctAnswers.Abstract Factory: Provides an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes
correctAnswers.Adapter: Converts the interface of a class into another interface that clients expect
correctAnswers.Prototype: Creates new objects by cloning an existing instance instead of instantiating from scratch
correctAnswers.Strategy: Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime

explanation: Each pattern's intent is its textbook one-line purpose.

questionText: In a vending-machine controller, the same coin-insert and button-press events must behave differently depending on whether the machine is Idle, HasMoney, Dispensing or SoldOut, with explicit transitions between these modes. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Behavior that changes with an explicit internal mode plus defined transitions is the State pattern.

questionText: Complete the Strategy pattern: Navigator delegates to a swappable RouteStrategy.
explanation: The context holds a strategy reference, lets it be replaced, and delegates the algorithm to it.

matchingOptions: Shares common intrinsic state between many fine-grained objects to save memory
Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
Provides an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes
Encapsulates a request as an object, allowing parameterization, queuing, and undo

questionText: A game must spawn many enemies that are near-identical to a heavily configured prototype, copying it rather than re-running expensive setup each time. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Creating new objects by cloning a configured instance is the Prototype pattern.

questionText: Complete the Strategy pattern: Cart delegates to a swappable DiscountStrategy.
explanation: The context holds a strategy reference, lets it be replaced, and delegates the algorithm to it.

questionText: Match each design pattern to the intent that best describes it.
matchingOptions: Provides sequential access to the elements of a collection without exposing its underlying representation
Shares common intrinsic state between many fine-grained objects to save memory
Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime
Allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class
Centralizes communication between a set of objects so they no longer refer to each other directly

correctAnswers.Strategy: Defines a family of interchangeable algorithms and makes them selectable at runtime

questionText: A shape-drawing tool must create shape objects based on a string type read from a file ('circle', 'square'), centralising the creation logic in one place even though it is not a full GoF creational pattern. Which single approach is described?
explanation: Centralising creation in one method/class that switches on a parameter is the Simple Factory idiom.

questionText: Complete the Decorator: MilkDecorator wraps a Coffee and adds Milk.
explanation: A decorator implements the same component interface, holds a wrappee, and adds behavior around the delegated call.

Creates new objects by cloning an existing instance instead of instantiating from scratch
Provides a unified, simplified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem
Centralizes communication between a set of objects so they no longer refer to each other directly

questionText: An order-processing pipeline must run a fixed sequence — validate payment, register order, ship — identical for every order type, but a notification to a courier should optionally be added on top without modifying the pipeline. Which design patterns best fit? Select 1 or more answers
explanation: The fixed step sequence is Template Method; the optional courier add-on layered on top is Decorator.

questionText: Complete the Decorator: HoneyDecorator wraps a Beverage and adds Honey.
explanation: A decorator implements the same component interface, holds a wrappee, and adds behavior around the delegated call.

questionText: A spreadsheet must recalculate and visually update many dependent cells and charts the instant a source cell changes. Which single design pattern is the most appropriate?
explanation: Automatically updating many dependents when one subject changes is the Observer pattern.

questionText: Complete the Decorator: BoldDecorator wraps a TextView and adds Bold.
